description: Does a cited authority's holding SUPPORT the proposition it is cited for? (the Ayinde misstated-authority failure). Each case pairs a proposition with a REAL authority (holding looked up from data/legal_authorities.json) and a ground-truth expectFaithful. Scored by agent.legal_faithfulness with one or more LLM judges; a VALIDATED number requires the no-overclaim gate (>=2 independent judges, kappa>=0.40, >=3 runs, CI lower bound above chance). Unlike the objective citation-existence benchmark, this is a model-judged task — a single judge is illustrative only.

cases.proposition: The prorogation of Parliament was unlawful.
The court held that prorogation was lawful and within the prerogative.
Same-sex couples have a fundamental right to marry under the Fourteenth Amendment.
This authority bars all immigration appeals.
Sanctions were imposed for submitting fabricated, AI-generated case citations.
The court established the standard for airline liability for delayed baggage.
The court criticised AI-drafted submissions for making allegations without an evidential basis.
The court endorsed the severe allegations as proven on the evidence.
